fix_priority_order.fix_approach: The page_additions file defines locators in an Elements class as string selectors. According to the AURA framework Pattern B, when locators are stored as strings in an Elements class, they must be accessed via page.locator(page_obj.elements.selector_name). The test script incorrectly defines them as direct properties in __init__ (self.locator = page.locator(...)). Fix by: (1) Remove all property definitions from the page_additions __init__ section, (2) Update every usage in the test script to use page.locator(print_history_page.elements.{locator_name}) instead of print_history_page.{locator_name}. This is the highest priority fix as it affects 16 separate usages throughout the script.

Precondition 4 requires pausing the subscription plan before test execution. The script has a TODO comment indicating incomplete implementation. Implement the pause plan logic using existing GeminiRAHelper methods: access_edit_menu, select appropriate pause event/state, save changes, and verify the subscription state is 'paused'. This is critical because without a paused plan, the entire test scenario is invalid.

Steps 10 and 12 require simulating specific page counts (6 pages, then 9 more pages). The script has TODO comments indicating uncertainty about how to specify page count in send_rtp_devicestatus. Review the send_rtp_devicestatus implementation to understand how page counts are embedded in the telemetry data. Ensure the method is called correctly to register the specific page counts needed for the test. Add wait time after each call to allow backend processing.

Remove the unused import 'from core.utils import save_full_page_screenshot'. While the script uses this method in Step 20, the mapping report does not show it as a matched method for this test case, and visual verification is not a required test step per the test case analysis. Remove the import and the screenshot capture code in Step 20.
Change the timeout value in the not_to_be_visible assertion from 10000ms to 30000ms to match framework standards for consistency.

Encapsulate the pause plan precondition logic into a single helper method GeminiRAHelper.pause_subscription_plan(page, tenant_email) that handles all Rails Admin navigation and state changes internally. This improves code maintainability and follows the framework pattern of encapsulating admin operations in helper methods.
